payDisclaimer: These are benchmarks from spot-checked postings and general market data as of mid-2026, not guarantees. Always confirm pay on the live posting.

zones.name: Inner Loop core
zones.whatsThere: Healthcare IT & help desk (TMC hospitals), corporate HQ support desks, university IT
zones.transit: Best. Served by all three light-rail lines (Red / Green / Purple) and most bus routes; Park & Ride express buses end here
zones.payNotes: Healthcare and university desks; steady volume, transit-accessible

zones.name: Uptown / Galleria / Westchase
zones.whatsThere: Corporate office IT / help desk, finance & insurance support
zones.transit: Decent near the Galleria (frequent buses + Uptown dedicated bus lanes); Westchase is more car-oriented
zones.payNotes: Corporate, finance, and insurance support desks

zones.name: Energy Corridor / Katy / West
zones.whatsThere: Large corporate IT & service desks (bp, Shell, ConocoPhillips, Baker Hughes, Fluor)
zones.transit: Park & Ride (Addicks) express to downtown; otherwise car-dependent
zones.payNotes: Corporate and contract IT; contract rates run higher

zones.name: North / The Woodlands / Spring
zones.whatsThere: Corporate campuses (ExxonMobil, HPE), airport & logistics IT
zones.transit: Park & Ride from Spring / Kingwood; mostly car-dependent
zones.payNotes: Corporate campuses; some higher contract roles

zones.name: Southeast / Clear Lake / Bay Area
zones.whatsThere: Aerospace & government contractors, petrochemical IT, insurance service desks (e.g., American National in Galveston)
zones.transit: Bay Area Park & Ride to downtown / TMC; otherwise car-dependent
zones.payNotes: Aerospace and government contractors often pay above retail help desk

zones.name: Southwest / Sugar Land / Fort Bend
zones.transit: Park & Ride from Sugar Land / Missouri City; car-dependent

alsoCheckAnywhere: Triple-S Steel Supply (in-house IT help desk)
Harris County (confirm pay carefully, some start below $19/hr)
recentExampleListings: Energy Corridor: C2O — IT Support Technician (part-time), starting around $22/hr
Inner Loop / TMC: UTHealth Houston — Systems & Applications Specialist supporting clinical IT
Southwest / Sugar Land: Sun Com Mobile — IT Technical Support Specialist, about $45K-$55K/yr (roughly $22-$26/hr)
Apprenticeship-style role: Houston IT Apprentice postings have run about $26-$31/hr
Statewide contract benchmark: Texas entry-level help desk contract roles have posted around $24-$27/hr
otherMetroMethod: List the major job clusters — search '[your city] largest employers' and '[your city] business districts'.
Group them into 4-6 zones by geography (e.g., 'downtown/core', 'north suburbs', 'west office parks').
Mark transit access for each zone using your local transit agency's rail/bus map and Park & Ride lots.
Set a commute cap and pick the 1-2 zones you can realistically reach (or go remote-first if transportation is limited).
Search each zone by name or ZIP using the Search Toolkit strings, swapping in your city.
